description: This Capability Statement defines the expected capabilities of a Personal Functioning and Engagement FHIR server conforming to the Personal Functioning and Engagement Implementation Guide.
name: Personal_Functioning_And_Engagement_CapabilityStatement

rest.documentation:
The PFE Server **SHALL**:

1. Support all profiles defined in this Implementation Guide.
2.  Implement the RESTful behavior according to the FHIR specification.
3. Return the following response classes:
   - (Status 400): invalid parameter
   - (Status 401/4xx): unauthorized request
   - (Status 403): insufficient scope
   - (Status 404): unknown resource
   - (Status 410): deleted resource.
4. Support json source formats for all PFE interactions.
5. Support the searchParameters on each profile  individually and in combination.

The PFE  Server **SHOULD**:

1. Support xml source formats for all PFE interactions.

searchParam.documentation: Patient information, if the resource is affixed to a person
searchParam.name: patient

searchParam.documentation: Individual the service is ordered for
searchParam.name: patient

searchParam.documentation: Obtained date/time. If the obtained element is a period, a date that falls in the period
searchParam.name: date

searchParam.documentation: The classification of the type of diagnostic report
searchParam.name: category

searchParam.documentation: The code of the report type
searchParam.name: code

searchParam.documentation: The subject that the report is about (if patient)
searchParam.name: patient

searchParam.documentation: Obtained date/time. If the obtained element is a period, a date that falls in the period
searchParam.name: date

searchParam.documentation: The code of the observation type
searchParam.name: code

searchParam.documentation: The subject that the observation is about (if patient)
searchParam.name: patient

searchParam.documentation: The classification of the type of observation
searchParam.name: category

searchParam.documentation: A server defined search that may match any of the string fields in the HumanName, including family, given, prefix, suffix, and/or text
searchParam.name: name

security.description:
1. See the [Guidance](security_and_data_sharing.html) section for requirements and recommendations.
1. A server **SHALL** reject any unauthorized requests by returning an HTTP 401 "Unauthorized", HTTP 403 "Forbidden", or HTTP 404 "Not Found" response code.

title: Personal Functioning and Engagement Capability Statement
